


## **Installation**
The Clue application may be ran through any IDE that is capable of interpreting Python. For these instructions, Group 5 is using Visual Studio Code to run the program, which was used throughout the development process.

Follow these steps to run and install the Clue application:

1. Download the zip file from the GitHub repository
   <img width="921" height="401" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 011754" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/06244f9c-33df-40b7-8e9e-a131b13ae788" />

3. Extract the ZIP folder in your desired location  
4. Open the main.py file in your desired IDE  
5. Run the python file

## **Player Turns**

The game requires 4 people to play. There is no option for less people or an AI player to replace one or more players at this current stage. 

Each turn will follow the same pattern:

- Player rolls dice  
- Player can move the determined  
- Player can make suggestion if in a room  
- Player has option to make accusation  
- Player ends turn

# **Understanding the UI** 

<img width="1139" height="810" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 012453" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14bbdbfb-05f1-4228-b3f1-ebf6e986d67e" />


Upon starting the game, the player is presented with this screen

*Roll Dice* will roll the player’s movement dice for the turn and display that number in the Moves spot. As the player begins their movement, that number will show the remaining moves left for their turn. Players can move using the arrow keys on the UI, or the arrow keys on their keyboard  
<img width="65" height="26" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 013536" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/eaf7316b-e600-41a0-9167-2c27a74c91d1" />


*Your Cards* shows the cards in the specified player's hand. As in the traditional Clue boardgame, these cards are not in the solution.

The *Detective Notepad* is separate to each player, allowing them to mark/unmark card discoveries during their turn. They do not mark automatically, requiring the player to manually select/unselect the desired boxes. 

<img width="185" height="138" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 013324" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2a1c10c2-d17a-4d33-96fd-b68fe528a3d6" />
<img width="200" height="140" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 013343" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5abb57e1-6e19-41a9-b365-f205c7bcc4cb" />


Once a player selects *End Turn*, their turn ends and the next player’s begins.

## **Making a Suggestion** 

If a player enters one of the rooms, they have the option to make a suggestion, using that room as the location of the crime. They can do that by interacting with the *Make Suggestion* option.

This popup will appear, allowing the player to select a potential Suspect and Weapon   
<img width="237" height="194" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 013823" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a97b1dd4-f951-4520-9ce8-88892cd2c9b4" />


If the suggestion has an incorrect card, the player will be randomly shown a card from another player’s hand  
<img width="279" height="32" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 014207" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/65fb7544-2ac8-4a7b-8b12-f6a35c965e01" />


If no cards are present that can refute the suggestion, the player is instead told that  
<img width="212" height="24" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 014451" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/265ca8d9-ac9c-455e-bb5a-1b100f5c179d" />


A player cannot make an accusation in the same turn as a suggestion

## **Making an Accusation** 

If a player believes they have solved the crime, they can select *Make Accusation* anytime after rolling for movement on their turn, as long as they are in the room. Unlike making a suggestion, they do not need to be in the same room they believe the crime to be committed in to make the accusation.  
<img width="225" height="184" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 014741" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7c8eead9-9296-4245-a679-8d3e7eb5da7b" />


If the accusation is wrong, the player is told as much, and they are removed from the turn order  
<img width="447" height="29" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 014803" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/cde56e9e-f522-49ff-b556-00af6e45a4ad" />


If the accusation is correct, the player is told as much, and they win the game  
<img width="313" height="30" alt="Screenshot 2026-02-26 014906" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/19345a7c-8cce-45ed-b0bc-5e6099875705" />
